name: Catonsville MD, USA

rent.notes: Baltimore County — mature tree-lined neighborhoods along Frederick Road corridor, historic Oella (mill village) to south. UMBC (University of Maryland, Baltimore County) + Catonsville Community College keep area vibrant.

groceries.notes: Wegmans, Giant, Harris Teeter, Trader Joe's, H Mart (Korean). Strong grocery variety for the price point.

utilities.notes: BGE electric + Washington Gas + Baltimore County DPW water/sewer.

healthcare.notes: Medicare baseline. UM St. Agnes Hospital 5 min, Johns Hopkins campus 15 min, UM Medical Center (Baltimore) 15 min. Exceptional specialist density.

transportation.notes: MTA (Maryland Transit Administration) bus to Baltimore. Light Rail 15 min (via car). Interstate 95/Interstate 695 access. BWI (Baltimore-Washington International airport) 20 min. No direct MARC station; Penn Line at Baltimore Penn Station 15 min.

entertainment.notes: Walkable Frederick Road commercial strip, Patapsco Valley State Park trails, UMBC (University of Maryland, Baltimore County) events + Fine Arts shows, Catonsville Arts District. Cable+1Gig typical.

miscellaneous.notes: Fewer HOAs (homeowners associations) than newer MD suburbs. Interstate 95 + ICC (Intercounty Connector toll road) tolls. Baltimore County piggyback 3.20%.

medicalOOP.notes: Same retiree profile. World-class specialist access: UM St. Agnes in town, Johns Hopkins + UM Medical Center 15 min. One of the best healthcare locations for complex retirees.

taxes.notes: Income tax computed from MD + Baltimore County brackets.

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (Baltimore County, MD). Maryland Health Connection — Baltimore metro competitive marketplace. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: MD pension exclusion up to ~$39K for retirees 65+; SS fully exempt. Combined MD state + Baltimore County piggyback (3.20%).

propertyTax.notes: Renters: no direct property tax. Maryland has no vehicle personal property tax.

taxes.notes: MD tax benefits (Social Security exempt, pension exclusion, no vehicle tax). Baltimore County piggyback 3.20%.

healthcare.notes: Outstanding healthcare density: UM St. Agnes Hospital in town (5 min), Johns Hopkins Bayview + Hopkins main campus 15-20 min, UM Medical Center (Baltimore) 15 min, GBMC 20 min. Excellent for complex retiree medical needs.

acaMarketplace.notes: Maryland Health Connection — Baltimore County rating area (competitive metro marketplace).
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

lifestyle.notes: Walkable Frederick Road commercial district with local restaurants, breweries, and the historic Catonsville Lumber. Patapsco Valley State Park trails literally in town. UMBC (University of Maryland, Baltimore County) campus cultural events. Strong schools and mature tree-lined neighborhoods. Historic Oella mill village walkable from downtown.

pros: ~$2,500/mo cheaper than Fairfax while keeping walkable-town character
MD tax benefits: Social Security exempt, pension exclusion, no vehicle property tax
World-class healthcare (St. Agnes in town, Johns Hopkins + UM 15 min)
Walkable Frederick Road commercial strip — real pedestrian life
Patapsco Valley State Park trails in town
UMBC (University of Maryland, Baltimore County) campus = cultural events, continuing education, Fine Arts
Mature tree-lined neighborhoods, less strip-mall feel than outer MD suburbs
cons: Baltimore County piggyback 3.20% — tied for highest in MD
No direct MARC station (15 min to Penn Station)
~55 mi from Fairfax — longer drive than Bowie
Baltimore metro reputation concerns (though Catonsville itself is safe)
